name: Leap Year
description: February 29, leaplings, the Julian and Gregorian calendars, and why we need an extra day every four years.

questions.q: On what date does the extra day fall in a leap year?
How many days are in a common (non-leap) year in the Gregorian calendar?
How many days does February have in a leap year?
How often does a leap year occur in the Gregorian calendar (basic rule)?

source.notes: With exceptions for centurial years (see other questions).

questions.q: Who introduced the original 'every-4-years' leap year system in 46 BC?

source.notes: Created the Julian calendar with help from astronomer Sosigenes of Alexandria.

questions.q: Which pope reformed the Julian calendar in 1582 to create the Gregorian calendar still in use today?

source.notes: Issued the bull Inter gravissimas in 1582.

questions.q: What additional rule did the Gregorian calendar add to the Julian system?
choices: Add a leap day every 5 years instead of 4
Skip leap year in centurial years not divisible by 400
Add a leap month every 19 years
Skip leap year in years ending in 7

source.original_a: He adjusted the leap year rule to skip leap years in centuries not divisible by 400
source.notes: So 1900 was NOT a leap year; 2000 WAS. This corrects the Julian calendar's slight overshoot.

questions.q: Approximately how long does Earth take to orbit the Sun?

source.notes: The fractional part is why we need leap years; the Gregorian rule averages 365.2425.

questions.q: What is the affectionate term for someone born on February 29?
Which Texas town has branded itself the 'Leap Year Capital of the World'?

source.notes: Anthony — straddling the Texas–New Mexico border — has hosted a Leap Year Festival since 1988.

questions.q: In Irish folklore, what 'privilege' is traditionally granted to women on February 29?
choices: They can take the day off work
They can propose marriage to a man

source.original_a: A day when women can propose marriage
source.notes: Sometimes called 'Bachelor's Day' or 'Lady's Privilege'. Original answer mis-labeled this as 'Sadie Hawkins Day' — Sadie Hawkins is a separate, U.S.-comic-strip tradition typically observed in November, not on Feb 29.

questions.q: Why was February historically chosen as the month to receive the extra leap day?
choices: It honors the Roman god February
It avoided affecting religious feast days

source.notes: Going back to ancient Roman calendar reforms — February was already the runt of the year.

questions.q: Was the year 1900 a leap year in the Gregorian calendar?

source.original_a: He adjusted the leap year rule to skip leap years in centuries not divisible by 400
source.notes: 1900 is divisible by 100 but not 400 → skipped. 2000 IS divisible by 400 → leap year.

questions.q: Will 2100 be a leap year in the Gregorian calendar?
choices: Only in the Eastern Orthodox calendar

source.original_a: He adjusted the leap year rule to skip leap years in centuries not divisible by 400
source.notes: Divisible by 100 but not 400 → not a leap year. The next century leap year is 2400.

questions.q: How many days are in the Ethiopian calendar's 13th month, Pagumē, in a leap year?

source.original_a: It adds an extra day to the 13th month
source.notes: Pagumē usually has 5 days; gains a 6th in leap years.

questions.q: Which major sporting event reliably falls in leap years?

source.notes: Modern Olympics generally on leap years (with rare exceptions like 2020-postponed-to-2021).

questions.q: Which U.S. quadrennial event is also held in leap years?

source.notes: Held every 4 years on leap years; the Census is every 10 (years ending in 0).

questions.q: Approximately how much extra time accumulates each year that requires a leap day to correct?

source.notes: Earth's solar year is 365 days, 5 hours, 48 minutes, 46 seconds — close to 6 hours per year × 4 years ≈ 1 day.

questions.q: In what year of the 21st century did February 29 fall on a Monday?
Approximately what are the odds of being born on February 29?

source.notes: 1 day out of 1,461 days in 4 years = ~0.0684%.
